description: Results for 10 new benchmark cases (Issue #7 — convergence sample size expansion)

ceiling_analysis.differentiating_dimension: ETD only — baseline IDR/IDP/FVC all 1.0 on non-ceiling cases; ETD=0.5 due to underspecified test criteria vs debate ETD=1.0

issue_7_finding: With new cases: easy=10, medium=9 (unchanged), hard=11 (from 7+new). Convergence is 1.0 on all new cases across both difficulty tiers — no evidence that harder cases reduce convergence.
issue_11_finding: Debate ceiling remains unbroken (10/10 new cases at 1.0). Baseline breaks ceiling on 2/10 hard cases (rw003, si004) via ETD=0.5. Ceiling effect is a debate-protocol property, not a benchmark-difficulty property.

cases.convergence_notes: Critic (C1, C2) and Defender independently identified preprocessing_pipeline_mismatch and attribution_error. Both proposed equivalent empirical remedies. No contested points.
cases.judge_notes: Full convergence. Both agents independently reached the same diagnosis and proposed the same empirical test: evaluate production model under its native preprocessing; 2x2 factorial to isolate preprocessing from model contribution.

cases.convergence_notes: Critic and Defender independently identified post_hoc_threshold_tuning and train_test_contamination. Both proposed validation-set-based threshold selection. No contested points.
cases.judge_notes: Full convergence. Agreed test: select threshold on validation set only, evaluate both models at pre-committed thresholds on test set, compare to original test-optimized figures. AUROC comparison provides threshold-independent sanity check.

cases.convergence_notes: Critic concluded critique_wins via Goodhart's Law mechanism and selective reporting. Defender independently concluded critique_wins after noting A/B controls for confounds and that post-hoc data is directionally unambiguous.
cases.judge_notes: No contested points. Post-hoc data (session length -22.6%, 7-day return -12.2pp) is present, controlled (A/B), and unaddressed by team. ETD null — evidence already in hand.

cases.convergence_notes: Critic raised four issues pointing to macro-averaging masking class-level heterogeneity. Defender independently identified the same core structural flaw. Both specified identical test: per-class F1 breakdown for all 12 intents.
cases.judge_notes: Full convergence. Both agents independently proposed identical empirical test. Per-class F1 breakdown for all 12 intents; if any tail intent degraded, 'across all intents' claim is false.

cases.convergence_notes: Critic examined four concern areas (leakage, baselines, evaluation horizon, metric) and found no genuine flaws. Defender independently stressed same four dimensions. Full convergence on defense_wins.
cases.judge_notes: No contested points. All design elements hold: temporal split, baseline choice, evaluation horizon, metric selection. Improvement margins (12% over seasonal naive) are meaningful.

cases.convergence_notes: Critic examined six concern areas and found none genuine. Defender independently identified same design strengths. One open question (distributional shift magnitude unspecified) correctly assessed by both as not undermining stated claim.
cases.judge_notes: Full convergence. Prospective frozen model, external demographic cohort, pre-registered targets — all standard objections addressed. Distributional shift magnitude limitation noted but does not undermine stated claim.

cases.convergence_notes: Critic enumerated five candidate critiques and correctly found none material. Defender Pass 1 found no critical flaws; defense_wins verdict consistent with reasoning. No contested points.
cases.judge_notes: Full convergence. Conservative claim, transparent reporting, standard framework, all datasets reported including losses. Critic correctly avoided manufacturing critiques.

cases.convergence_notes: All four agents independently identified both must-find issues (label_leakage_via_temporal_ordering, outcome_feedback_loop). Defender in v2 isolation reproduced both core findings. Proposed empirical test (response latency ablation) consistent across all agents.
cases.judge_notes: Textbook outcome-contaminated feature case. Routing system priority assignment is itself a latent escalation predictor; response latency is a noisy readout. The team described the mechanism in the prompt but did not name it as a confound. Both agents caught it independently.

cases.convergence_notes: Critic and Defender independently identified both must_find issues (deployment_population_mismatch, self_selection_bias_in_training_labels) without coordination. Baseline also identified both issues but did not specify a concrete falsifiable test with pass/fail criteria.
cases.defender_verdict: critique — same structural objections reached independently
cases.judge_notes: Full convergence on both must-find issues. Empirical tests: (1) collect outcome labels on random sample of historically rejected applicants, measure model AUC on that cohort — if AUC degrades toward 0.5, mismatch confirmed; (2) multilevel model decomposing first-year rating variance by manager vs. individual fixed effects — if manager variance dominates, label validity is undermined. Baseline ETD=0.5: gestured at remedies but did not specify falsifiable test conditions.

cases.convergence_notes: Critic and Defender independently identified optimization_target_scope_mismatch and local_optimum_vs_system_objective. Baseline also identified both issues but did not specify what metric result on a pilot would confirm or disconfirm scope mismatch.
cases.defender_verdict: critique — independently identified per-route vs fleet-level scope mismatch and sim-to-real gap
cases.judge_notes: Full convergence on both must-find issues. Empirical tests: (1) run all 200 agents simultaneously in simulation and compare fleet-level aggregate cost vs reported 18% per-route gain — if fleet gain is materially smaller, scope mismatch confirmed; (2) compare simulation travel time distribution to production historical data; (3) staged pilot on 10-20 vehicles would resolve both simultaneously. Baseline ETD=0.5: suggested pilot but did not specify what metric outcome would confirm/disconfirm the hypothesis.
